notes: Spec asserts: ready → working, state stays working across all retry cycles (no flicker to ready or waiting between attempts), final working → ready. The codex recipe drives a deterministic failing-then-corrected exec sequence (cat a nonexistent file, then fall back to ls) in ONE turn: the codex parser sees one user_message, a failing function_call/function_call_output pair, a corrective function_call/function_call_output pair, then a task_complete (turn_done). A failing function_call_output still CLOSES the open call (HasOpenToolCall clears) so the daemon never treats the tool failure as a waiting gate; task_complete is the only ready settle. The intermediate_working phase pins the no-flicker invariant with duration_at_least_ms 2000 — long enough to span both exec cycles given typical codex latency. If the parser ever surfaced an errored tool_result as a transition to ready or waiting, this phase would fire.

text: User prompt drives ready → working (spec bullet 1)
State stays working across the failing exec and the corrective exec — the errored function_call_output must NOT flicker the session to ready or waiting before the recovery cycle (spec bullet 2)
After the final successful attempt: working → ready (spec bullet 3)
